ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT HENKEL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT DID NOT SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. BY FAILING TO ATTACH PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIALLY PREPARED CONTENTION DISCOVERY WITH ITS MOVING PAPERS, DEFENDANT CANNOT SUCCESSFULLY INVOKE THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO MR. LEHMANN'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED HIS CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: HELEN SOREP-MATLEY, CSR 	225. (220)

***Brayton Group 578, Lease Case #274139, Teale vs. Asbestos Defendants***

APPEARANCES:
James Nevin and Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.
Joseph Adams of Bassi, Edlin, Huie & Blum representing JT Thorpe & Son, Inc.
Jon C. James of Hassard Bonnington representing Sequoia Ventures, Inc. (Stone, Owens and Walsh cases)
Douglas Kroesch of Gallaws representing Baltimore Aircoil (Teale case)
Dana Burch of Walsworth, Franklin, et al. representing Thomas Dee Engineering Co.
Ian Dillon of Walsworth, Franklin, et al. representing Westburne Supply, Inc.
Jennifer Juden and Eduardo Robles of DeHay & Elliston representing Kaiser Gypsum (Walsh case)

Defendant Crane Co. has resolved out of all of the cases. Brayton Group 578 (wrongful death) will goes to trial first.

At 3:14 p.m., court and counsel confer on the juror questionnaire to be used for hardship as well as a proposed joint statement of the case. There are no motions in limine that need to be heard before voir dire. Juror hardships will be conducted 12/14/11, voir dire on 12/19/11 and 12/20/11, motions in limine will be heard on 1/3/12 and trial will commence on 1/4/12.

The court recess from 3:24 p.m. to 3:37 p.m. in order for counsel to finalize the juror questionnaire.

Court and counsel confer on the questionnaire. Copies of the finalized version must be provided to the court by tomorrow (12/14/11) at 9:00 a.m. in Department 602. Counsel have also finalized a generic statement of the case. Counsel estimate the trial will last six weeks including deliberations. There will be 20 days worth of evidence. All counsel are ordered present for jury hardshipping. Plaintiffs' counsel is allotted one hour for voir dire and defense counsel will confer on possibly conducting a joint voir dire with their time.

Court and counsel confer on deposition designation and counter-designations. The court orders a briefing schedule on it. Counsel in living asbestos cases should also abide by all deadlines set out in today's proceedings.

At 3:57 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel L. Martens, Esq., of DAL SAGLIO & MARTENS LLP (310-662-4748), appearing for defendant / moving party Aerojet-General Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James E. Owens

RULING:
DEFENDANT AEROJET-GENERAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Aerojet-General Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to produce admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the portions of plaintiff's declaration regarding products attributable to defendant other than adhesive products are sustained on the basis of the D'Amico rule. Defendant's objection to exhibit 2 to Ms. Meyer's declaration is sustained on grounds of lack of authentication. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jessica A. Fakhimi, Esq., of CHAPMAN & INTRIERI, LLP (510-864-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Henkel Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James E. Owens

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENKEL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Henkel Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is off calendar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. By failing to attach plaintiff's responses to specially prepared contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Lehmann's declaration are sustained. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225 = (220/HEK)
